coredata.dc:title: Cobalt-iron mixed-metal-organic framework (Co3Fe-MMOF) with high oxidase mimicking activity for sensitive colorimetric detection of glutathione
coredata.prism:publicationName: Journal of Food Composition and Analysis
coredata.dc:creator: Wei, Xiuxia
Qi, Shuyan
Fu, Tingting
Zhu, Haiguang
Meng, Zheyan
Gong, Wanqing
Qiu, Jingxuan
Xu, Dongpo
coredata.dc:description: In this work, Co3Fe-MMOF nanoparticles with regular nanodiamond morphology were synthesized by a solvothermal method, and glutathione (GSH) was detected by a colorimetric method. By optimizing the Co3Fe-MMOF oxide mimetic enzyme system for the detection of GSH to determine the detection limit and anti-interference ability with a detection time of 15 min, we found a good linear relationship between the absorbance value and GSH concentration in the range of 0.012–0.2 mM (linear equation y = −5.176x + 1.159, R2 of 0.9907), and the detection limit ( LOD) was 0.07 mM, and the detection of GSH in actual sample milk was successfully achieved.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Oxidase activity
Glutathione
Colorimetric detection
Co3Fe-MMOF

xocs:meta.xocs:srctitle: Journal of Food Composition and Analysis

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Colorimetric detection of GSH by Co3Fe-MMOF
Synthesis and characterization of the Co3Fe-MMOF
Catalysis of Co3Fe-MMOF and the detection of GSH
Catalytic oxidation performance of Co3Fe - MMOF
Analysis of the colorimetric sensor in food samples

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Synthesis and catalytic application diagram of nanodiamond morphology Co3Fe-MMOF.
SEM images of (a) Co3Fe-MMOF, (b) Fe-MOF and (c) Co-MOF nanomaterials.
The effect of the reaction conditions with (a) pH, (b) temperature, (c) reaction time.
The effect of the reaction conditions with (a) the concentration of TMB, (b) the concentration of H2O2.
Colorimetric reaction using Co3Fe-MMOF as catalyst with different concentrations.
Colorimetric detection of GSH with different concentrations using Co3Fe-MMOF as catalyst.
The selectivity of the method using Co3Fe-MMOF as catalyst for colorimetric detection of GSH with different disturbed target objects.

head.ce:title: Cobalt-iron mixed-metal-organic framework (Co3Fe-MMOF) with high oxidase mimicking activity for sensitive colorimetric detection of glutathione

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Health Science and Engineering, University of Shanghai for Science and Technology, Shanghai 200093, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Health Science and Engineering, University of Shanghai for Science and Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Health Science and Engineering, University of Shanghai for Science and Technology, Shanghai 200093, PR China

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Correspondence to: School of Health Science and Engineering, University of Shanghai for Science and Technology, 516 Jungong Road, Yangpu District, Shanghai 200093, PR China.

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Health Science and Engineering, University of Shanghai for Science and Technology

ce:footnote.ce:note-para: Xiuxia Wei and Shuyan Qi contributed equally in this work.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: In this work, Co3Fe-MMOF nanoparticles with regular nanodiamond morphology were synthesized by a solvothermal method, and glutathione (GSH) was detected by a colorimetric method. By optimizing the Co3Fe-MMOF oxide mimetic enzyme system for the detection of GSH to determine the detection limit and anti-interference ability with a detection time of 15 min, we found a good linear relationship between the absorbance value and GSH concentration in the range of 0.012–0.2 mM (linear equation y = −5.176x + 1.159, R2 of 0.9907), and the detection limit ( LOD) was 0.07 mM, and the detection of GSH in actual sample milk was successfully achieved.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: No data was used for the research described in the article.

ce:section.ce:para: Glutathione ( GSH) is reported to be mostly present in biological cells and also is the most abundant sulfhydryl compound in cells (Guan et al., 2022; Xiao et al., 2021). Its concentration changes are closely related to aging, leucopenia, cardiovascular diseases (Jia et al., 2020), liver injury (Devaraj, 2020), cancer, HIV and neurodegeneration diseases (Gupta et al., 2016; Shamsipur et al., 2014). In recent years, GSH has been widely used in food, medicine, anti-aging and health care with the in-depth study of its physical and chemical properties. At present, a variety of analytical methods for the detection of glutathione have been established, such as electrochemistry, immunoluminescence, mass spectrometry, fluorescence, surface enhanced Raman scattering, electrochemiluminescence and high performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) (Fan et al., 2017; Niu et al., 2015; Qi et al., 2018; Tcherkas and Denisenko, 2001; Wang et al., 2016; Zhu et al., 2015; Zhang et al., 2018). Although these methods have high detection sensitivity, they have high requirements for the professional and cost of equipment and instruments and operators. Therefore, there is an urgent need to develop a rapid, convenient, sensitive and selective glutathione assay (Xiao, 2021).

Peroxidase is a kind of natural enzyme, which can catalyze many redox reactions such as 3,3,5,5-tetramethylbenzidine(TMB)-H2O2 reaction (Huang et al., 2020;Ren et al., 2018 and Shen et al., 2022). However, due to the shortcomings of peroxidase, such as poor stability, high process cost, difficult storage conditions and high denaturation risk, its application in analysis and diagnosis is limited. Compared with natural enzymes, oxidase mimics based on nanomaterials have attracted wide attention due to their simple synthesis, low cost, good stability and flexible design (Li et al., 2014).

Nanozymes make it possible for nanomaterials, such as MOF materials to replace natural enzymes, which can reduce costs, shorten time and have good stability. Zeng et al. (2018) designed a novel biosensor platform based on gas pressure detection of kanamycin by using platinum nanozyme to catalyze gas production on the framework of polyaniline nanowire functionalized reduced graphene oxide ( PANI / rGO). Yu et al.(2020) used a flexible pressure sensor constructed from three-dimensional ( 3D) polypyrrole ( PPy) foam to detect carcinoembryonic antigen ( CEA) sensitively and quickly. Yu et al.(2022) integrated a cascade enzymatic reaction device based on hollow Prussian blue nanoparticles ( h-PB NPs) and a signal visualization platform adapted for portable smartphones into an integrated 3D printing detection device.The detection of tyrosine catalyzed by Tb-MOF fluorescent nanozyme has been designed and synthesized (Wang and Chen, 2022). This luminescent nanozyme can be used to replace the combination of natural enzymes and chromogenic agents for enzyme-based determination, which provides a new idea for innovating various enzyme-based determinations. Some researchers used nanomaterials for biocatalysis and biosensors. For example, Ge et al. (2022) dispersed atoms and carried them by porous carbon derived from metal-organic frameworks, and designed a new colorimetric method based on Fe-N / CSAzymes for the determination of malathion. Nanomaterials have received increasing attention as enzyme mimics in chemistry, biomedicine, and biosensing. Zeng et al.(2020) combined SACs with DNA nanotechnology to open up new opportunities for protein diagnosis and biosafety. Yu et al.(2019) designed a novel multi-walled carbon nanotube modified paper electrode flexible pressure sensor for carcinoembryonic antigen ( CEA) digital multimeter reading immunoassay, which can be used for ultrasensitive detection of diseases in daily life. Based on the sensing platform of flexible pressure sensors and electrochromic devices, Yu et al.(2021) realized the visual detection of carcinoembryonic antigen ( CEA), which can be used for biological detection in health care sites and home medical environments. By using the peroxidase-like activity of CS@Fe/CDs to efficiently remove biofilms, Pan et al.(2022) provided a new strategy for artificial nanozymes in food safety and environmental protection.

Colorimetric detection is a simple and direct measurement method, which is widely used because of its high precision, fast response, low cost, simple operation and visualization (Rao et al., 2019). Colorimetric determination has been successfully applied in medical diagnosis, chemical sensing, food analysis, environmental monitoring and public safety (Ai and He, 2020). (Shen et al., 2023a,2023a) constructed an enzymatic cascade activation ratio fluorescence-colorimetric dual-mode system AOX / CeO2 @TPE+OPD to catalyze CH3SH. (Shen et al., 2023a,2023a) proposed a colorimetric and fluorescent dual-mode visual imaging sensor for OPs based on H2O2-free 2D nanozymes, which provides a broad prospect for ensuring environmental health and food safety. There are also smartphone-based colorimetric strategies for rapid detection of certain harmful substances (Ye et al., 2020). There are also colorimetric methods to detect heavy metal ions, such as mercury ions.

In this study, Co3Fe-MMOF nanoparticles with regular nanodiamond morphology were synthesized by solvothermal method, Co3Fe-MMOF nanomaterials with high catalytic oxidation activity can catalyze the reaction of TMB with H2O2, and the oxidation product of TMB ( oxTMB) was reduced to TMB by GSH, and the absorbance value gradually decreased at 652 nm with the increase of GSH concentration. The color of the reaction system gradually changed from dark blue to light and finally to colorless, which successfully established a rapid, convenient and highly sensitive colorimetric detection method for GSH and realized the application of GSH detection in actual samples (milk).

NaOH, NaCl, KCl, CaCl2, NaHc-HAc, CoCl2•6H2O, FeCl3•6H2O, N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF), 3,3,5,5-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB),anhydrous ethanol, H2O2, histidine, anhydrous ethanol, glucose, glycine, mannose, alanine, tyrosine, lysine, arginine, D-Alanine and glucose were purchased from Sinopharm (Shanghai, China). Terephthalic acid (BDC), pyrazine and terephthalic acid were obtained from J&K Scientific Ltd. All actual samples of milk were purchased from local supermarkets. All chemicals and reagents are the highest grade on the market and are used as is.

The synthesis method of Co3Fe-MMOF was based on the previous method in the laboratory(Xu et al., 2021). Dissolve CoCl2·6 H2O and FeCl3·6 H2O in a mixture of anhydrous ethanol and DMF, stir for 30 min, then add pyrazine and BDC, stir for 30 min at room temperature, transfer the solution to a 200 mL PTFE-lined stainless steel autoclave, heat at 120 ℃ for 10 h, then wash with DMF and ethanol for 3 times, and dry under vacuum at 50 ℃ to obtain Co3Fe-MMOF.
Co-MOF and Fe-MOF materials were synthesized according to the previously published method(Xu et al., 2021).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Colorimetric detection of GSH by Co3Fe-MMOF
ce:section.ce:para: By measuring the change of GSH concentration in the absorbance value of Co3Fe-MMOF at 652 nm, 100 μL of sodium acetate buffer, 100 μL of 1 mM TMB, 100 μL of 0.2 mg·mL−1 Co3Fe-MMOF and 100 μL of 1 mM H2O2 were added successively in a 1.5 mL centrifuge tube, and the color of the solution changed to dark blue before adding 100 μL of GSH of different concentrations was added and the reaction was carried out at room temperature for 15 min
Philips XL30 electron microscope from the Netherlands, Bruker D4 X-ray diffractometer and enzyme labeler (Spectra Max M2 MultiMode enzyme labeler, Molecular Devices, USA).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis and characterization of the Co3Fe-MMOF
ce:section.ce:para: The MOF nanomaterials were synthesized and characterized with the same results as the pre-synthesis in the laboratory (Xu et al., 2021). The synthesis process and catalytic applications of diamond shaped Co3Fe-MMOF nanomaterials are shown in Fig. 1 below. It is obvious from Fig. 2 that both Fe-MOF nanomaterials and Co3Fe-MMOF nanomaterials have very regular diamond shapes, while Co-MOF nanosheets have smooth surfaces and irregular shapes. Also Fig. S1 shows the XRD pattern of Co3Fe-MMOF nanomaterials, which confirms the structure of MOF compounds, and all the above results prove the successful synthesis of diamond-shaped Co3Fe-MMOF nanomaterials.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Catalysis of Co3Fe-MMOF and the detection of GSH
ce:section.ce:para: To investigate the catalytic performance of Co3Fe-MMOF, colorimetric reaction analysis was performed by using Co3Fe-MMOF as a catalyst for TMB.
The effects of reaction conditions, including pH, reaction temperature and reaction time, were considered in the experiments.

In this study, NaAc - HAc buffer solution was used to adjust the pH. As shown in Fig. 3a, the absorbance value increased with the increase of pH between 2 and 3.5, and decreased rapidly between pH 3.5–8, and reached the maximum value at pH = 3.5 with an obvious high absorption peak, indicating that Co3Fe-MMOF has good catalytic effect when used as a co-catalyst under acidic conditions.When the pH is too high, the reaction color gradually becomes transparent.For this reason, NaAc-HAc ( pH = 3.5) was chosen as the reaction buffer for the experiment. The effect of temperature on the reaction system can be seen in Fig. 3b. The absorbance values reached the maximum when the reaction temperature was close to 30 ℃, after which the absorbance values gradually decreased with the increase of temperature. As the reaction temperature increases, the color of the reaction system gradually transitions from green to yellow and then becomes transparent. Therefore, 30 ℃ is selected as the reaction temperature of the system. In addition, we also considered the effect of reaction time (Fig. 3c), the catalytic effect of Co3Fe-MMOF is very fast, and the color changed quickly when Co3Fe-MMOF nanomaterials were added to the reaction solution, and the absorbance value reached the maximum when the reaction time was close to 15 min and then gradually decreased, and finally 15 min was chosen as the reaction time for the experiment.

The effect of TMB concentration and H2O2 was also considered, as shown in Fig. 4a, the absorption value increased with the increase of TMB concentration below 1.2 mM, reached the maximum at 1.2 mM and then stabilized, the reaction color changed from yellow to green to blue, and the absorbance value reached the maximum when the H2O2 concentration reached 0.6 mM ( Fig. 4b). The catalytic kinetic curves of Co3Fe-MMOF shown in Fig. S2 indicate its good catalytic activity towards H2O2 and TMB.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Catalytic oxidation performance of Co3Fe - MMOF
ce:section.ce:para:
To investigate the catalytic performance of Co3Fe-MMOF, as shown in Fig. 5a, the blue color of the reaction system gradually deepened with the increase of the concentration of Co3Fe-MMOF nanomaterials, and the gradient of color change was obvious. The absorbance of the reaction system was also considered in the full wavelength scan, and Co3Fe-MMOF had the maximum absorption peak at 652 nm, and the absorbance varied significantly with different concentrations of Co3Fe-MMOF as catalysts, with good linearity in the range of 0.2 μg·mL−1 - 3.2 μg·mL−1 ( R2 = 0.9889). Also considering the catalytic oxidation mechanism of the reaction system, Fig. S3 gives a comparison of the catalytic oxidation performance of Co3Fe-MMOF, FeCl3
. 6 H2O, CoCl2
. 6 H2O and terephthalic acid at the same concentrations, it can be seen that Co3Fe-MMOF exhibits higher catalytic oxidation activity with obvious color changes, and all the above results can indicate that the nanodiamond shape of Co3Fe-MMOF material is an ideal catalyst. Fig. S4 shows that for other reaction systems, the reaction system of this experiment has higher reactivity.

The standard curve of GSH with nanodiamond-shaped Co3Fe-MMOF material as the catalyst was established as shown in Fig. 6a. The color of the reaction solution changed significantly from dark blue to lighter with increasing GSH concentration and had a good linearity. The limit of detection ( LOD) “3σ” ( σ = standard deviation of the measured value in the blank sample) of the detection system for GSH was 0.07 mM. The linear range of the assay was 0.012–0.2 mM, with an R2 as high as 0.9907. In addition, it can be seen that Table 1 reviews a number of assays for glutathione, many of which are sensitive but have relatively long detection times. Although the sensitivity of this study needs to be improved, the assay time is relatively fast and easy. Compared to other detection methods, colorimetric detection is more sensitive, does not require large instruments and equipment, low cost and time consuming.

In practical sample applications, the selectivity of the method is very important. As shown in Fig. 7, compared with the blank control experiment, 0.1 M Ca2+, Na+, K+, lysine, tyrosine, histidine, arginine, D-alanine and glycine could not discolor the color, 0.1 M glucose could cause little color change, and only 0.2 mM GSH discolored the color significantly. Therefore, the reaction system has good selectivity.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analysis of the colorimetric sensor in food samples
ce:section.ce:para: To demonstrate the feasibility of Co3Fe-MMOF in this reaction system, we analyzed the GSH in the actual sample milk by assaying it. The results in Table 2 show that the average recoveries of GSH were 95.75–110.5% and the relative standard deviations ( RSD) were 0.30–1.14%. The above evidence indicates that this colorimetric aptamer sensor has good practical applicability and can be used as an alternative method for the detection of GSH in real samples.

In summary, the present work provides a new colorimetric method for the sensitive detection of GSH. Co3Fe-MMOF can efficiently catalyze the reaction of TMB with H2O2, and the oxidation product of TMB ( oxTMB) is reduced to TMB by GSH. Co3Fe-MMOF material, as the catalyst in this reaction system, has high catalytic oxidation activity for the simple, sensitive and rapid detection of GSH, and the feasibility of this reaction system for the detection of GSH in actual sample milk has been demonstrated. Co3Fe-MMOF as a good oxidase The linear range of the detection was 0.012–0.2 mM with R2 up to 0.9907 and the detection limit was 0.07 mM. This system provides a powerful tool for the sensitive and rapid detection of GSH.

Xiuxia Wei: did the experiments, analysed data and wrote the paper. Shuyan Qi: did the experiments. Tingting Fu: did the experiments. Haiguang Zhu: analysed data. Zheyan Meng: analysed data. Qanqing Gong: analysed data. Dongpo Xu: reviewed and edited the paper. Jingxuan Qiu: supervised the research.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: We declare that we have no financial and personal relationships with other people or organizations that can inappropriately influence our work, there is no professional or other personal interest of any nature or kind in any product, service and/or company that could be construed as influencing the position presented in, or the review of, the manuscript entitled.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (32000470).

ce:section.ce:para: All authors have given approval to the final version of the manuscript.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data associated with this article can be found in the online version at doi:10.1016/j.jfca.2023.105472.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
Supplementary material.
.
